page: Vultr vhf-1c-1gb is a virtual machine belonging to the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 1 shared vCPU and 1.0 GB of RAM. The underlying hardware features an Intel x86_64 processor operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. This single-core, single-threaded instance includes 32 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU performance, the server achieves a score of 1865.79, placing it in the average tier. However, due to its single-core limitation, the multi-core stress-ng score remains identical at 1865.79, which falls into the bottom 10% tier. Memory performance is also modest, with cached small-block read bandwidth at 132.54 GB/sec and uncached large-block read and write bandwidths at 16.75 GB/sec and 13.63 GB/sec respectively, both ranking in the bottom 10%. Passmark CPU and memory scores further align with this entry-level tier.

From a resource density perspective, the vhf-1c-1gb offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for lightweight workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads or massive memory allocations. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and the 1.0 GB RAM limit, which restricts its utility for memory-intensive applications. However, with a static web serving throughput of 1.94 GB/sec and Redis SET performance at 2.19 million operations per second, this instance is well-suited for basic web serving, lightweight caching, microservices, and development or testing environments.
description: Vultr vhf-1c-1gb is an entry-level virtual server within the High Frequency Compute family, featuring 1 shared Intel x86_64 vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 32 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Designed for budget-conscious workloads, this single-core, single-threaded instance delivers average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1865.79, though its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower tiers. The server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. While the shared CPU allocation and limited memory present clear tradeoffs for resource-heavy tasks, the instance demonstrates capable throughput for lighter operations, achieving 1.94 GB/sec in static web serving and 2.19 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. It is best suited for low-traffic web servers, small-scale Redis caches, microservices, and basic development environments.
og_description: Vultr vhf-1c-1gb is a budget-friendly virtual server featuring 1 shared Intel vCPU, 1.0 GB RAM, and 32 GB NVMe SSD storage, ideal for basic web serving and development workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-1c-1gb is a shared-CPU virtual server with 1 Intel vCPU, 1.0 GB RAM, and 32 GB NVMe storage, designed for lightweight workloads.
tagline: An entry-level shared-CPU virtual instance featuring local NVMe storage for lightweight web serving and development environments.
bullet_points: Single-core Intel x86_64 processor with 1 shared vCPU and 1.0 GB RAM
Bundled 32 GB local NVMe SSD storage for fast read/write access
Average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1865.79
Includes one complimentary public IPv4 address
Suitable for lightweight web serving, Redis caching, and testing
